There is a pleasantness in conversations with servants of God which can make those who listen forget the pain and weariness of labor. Even the sons of the prophets must not be unwilling to work. Let no one think an honest job a burden or a disgrace. And mental work is as hard, and very often harder, than physical work. We should be careful with what we borrow, as if it were our own, because we must do as we would be done by. This man was so respectful of the axe head. And to those who have an honest mind, the worst thing about poverty is not so much their own lack and shame, as being unable to pay just debts. But the Lord cares for his people in their smallest concerns. And God’s grace can raise a hardened heart that is sunk into the mud of this world, and bring forth natural earthly affections.

The king of Israel regarded the warnings Elisha gave him about danger from the Syrians, but would not heed the warnings about danger from his sins. Such warnings are little heeded by most; they would save themselves from death, but will not from hell. Nothing that is done, said, or thought by any person in any place at any time is out of God’s knowledge.

What Elisha said to his servant is spoken to all faithful servants of God when there are fightings outside and fears within. Do not fear with a fear that causes torment and amazement; for those who are with us, to protect us, are more than those against us, to destroy us. His physical eyes were open, and he saw the danger with them. Lord, open the eyes of our faith so that we may see your protecting hand with them. The clearer sight we have of Heaven’s sovereignty and power, the less we will fear the troubles of earth. Satan, the god of this world, blinds men's eyes and deceives them to their own ruin; but when God enlightens their eyes, they see themselves in the midst of their enemies, captives to Satan, and in danger of hell, though before they thought their condition good. When Elisha had the Syrians at his mercy, he showed that he was influenced by both Divine goodness and Divine power. Let us not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good. The Syrians saw it was pointless to try to attack such a great and good man.

Learn to value plenty and to be thankful for it; see how contemptible money is when in time of famine it is freely given away for anything that is edible! Jehoram’s words to the woman may be the language of despair. See God's word fulfilled; among the threatenings of God’s judgments upon Israel for their sins, this was one: they would eat the flesh of their own children. The truth and awful justice of God were displayed in this horrible transaction. Alas! what miseries sin has brought upon the world! But foolishness perverts a man's way, and then his heart frets against the Lord. The king swears to put Elisha to death. Wicked men will blame someone else for their troubles rather than themselves, and will not leave their sins. If tearing clothes without a broken and contrite heart would help, or if wearing sackcloth without being renewed in spirit would serve, they would not stand out against the Lord. May the whole word of God increase in us reverent fear and holy hope so that we may be steadfast and immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that our labor is not in vain in the Lord.
